---
title: ""
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

```{=html}
<div class="page-header container">
  <p class="page-header-label">Work</p>
  <h1 class="page-header-title">Projects</h1>
  <p class="page-header-sub">
    Applied research, decision tools, and documented datasets. Each one is built
    around a real measurement question. The short classroom exercises live on the
    <a href="../teaching/">teaching page</a>.
  </p>
</div>

<div class="project-grid container">

  <div class="project-card">
    <span class="project-card-tag">Evaluation Practice · Decision Tool</span>
    <p class="project-card-title">Which Evaluation Should We Do?</p>
    <p class="project-card-desc">
      Say who needs to act on the findings, what the evaluation is for, how long
      the program has run, what data you can get, whether you have a comparison
      group, what you want to know, and whether the program follows an
      evidence-based model. Every approach then lands in one of three piles: what
      fits, what is the wrong tool and why, and what is close, with the gap named
      and the work to close it spelled out. It will tell you that cost-benefit is
      three steps away, and name the three steps.
    </p>
    <div class="project-card-meta">
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#8C6A8D;"></span>R / Quarto</span>
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#E98973;"></span>HTML / JS</span>
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#D2B1A3;"></span>Excel / readxl</span>
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#6b4f6c;"></span>Interactive</span>
    </div>
    <br>
    <a href="../evalpicker/eval_picker.html" style="font-weight:600; color:#E98973;">
      Open the evaluation picker →
    </a>
  </div>

  <div class="project-card">
    <span class="project-card-tag">Data Storytelling · Decision Tool</span>
    <p class="project-card-title">Which Format Fits Best?</p>
    <p class="project-card-desc">
      Say who you need to reach, how far the work has to travel, and what you need
      it to do. Every delivery format then lands in one of three piles: the ones
      that fit, the ones that could work once you adapt them, and the ones that
      are the wrong tool here and why. Built from the delivery-formats tree in
      "Cultivating a Court's Data STORY."
    </p>
    <div class="project-card-meta">
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#4F6B33;"></span>R / Quarto</span>
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#BC4634;"></span>HTML / JS</span>
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#C9A24B;"></span>Excel / readxl</span>
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#B0791C;"></span>Interactive</span>
    </div>
    <br>
    <a href="../formatpicker/format_picker.html" style="font-weight:600; color:#4F6B33;">
      Open the format picker →
    </a>
  </div>

  <div class="project-card">
    <span class="project-card-tag">Data Storytelling · Teaching Module</span>
    <p class="project-card-title">Hip-Hop Periodic Table</p>
    <p class="project-card-desc">
      A fully documented dataset of 173 hip-hop acts (129 solo artists and 44
      groups), each scored on five parts of lyricism, with confidence ratings
      for every act and a published data dictionary. Built to teach measurement
      validity, sampling bias, composite scoring, and uncertainty communication,
      using a subject people genuinely argue about.
    </p>
    <div class="project-card-meta">
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#8C6A8D;"></span>R / Quarto</span>
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#E98973;"></span>HTML / JS</span>
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#D2B1A3;"></span>Excel / readxl</span>
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#6b4f6c;"></span>Interactive</span>
    </div>
    <br>
    <a href="../hiphop/hiphop_periodic_table.html" style="font-weight:600; color:#8C6A8D;">
      Explore the periodic table →
    </a>
  </div>

  <div class="project-card">
    <span class="project-card-tag">Applied Research · Criminal Justice</span>
    <p class="project-card-title">Counted Wrong: The Line at 18</p>
    <p class="project-card-desc">
      A data essay built on Pathways to Desistance, a study that followed
      1,354 young people for seven years after a serious offense. Where does
      adolescence end and adulthood begin? The legal system draws a bright line
      at age 18, but human development does not. A project about what happens
      when a line someone drew gets treated as a fact of nature.
    </p>
    <div class="project-card-meta">
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#8C6A8D;"></span>R / Quarto</span>
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#E98973;"></span>Longitudinal data</span>
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#D2B1A3;"></span>Developmental measurement</span>
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#6b4f6c;"></span>Law &amp; policy</span>
    </div>
    <br>
    <a href="../countedwrong/index.html" style="font-weight:600; color:#8C6A8D;">
      Read the essay →
    </a>
  </div>

  <div class="project-card">
    <span class="project-card-tag">Applied Research · Aging &amp; the Law</span>
    <p class="project-card-title">A Right That Exists on Paper: Compassionate Release</p>
    <p class="project-card-desc">
      Nearly every state and the federal system has a compassionate or medical
      release law on the books: a way to release people who are dying, seriously
      ill, or simply too old to pose a risk. Almost none are used.
      Drawing on a fifty-state review and the data behind <em>Aging and the Criminal
      Legal System</em> (APA, 2025), this project measures the gap between a law
      that exists and a law that gets used, and asks what an unused law says
      about how a system measures mercy.
    </p>
    <div class="project-card-meta">
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#8C6A8D;"></span>R / Quarto</span>
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#E98973;"></span>50-state legal review</span>
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#D2B1A3;"></span>Aging &amp; corrections</span>
      <span><span class="project-card-accent" style="background:#6b4f6c;"></span>Law &amp; policy</span>
    </div>
    <br>
    <span class="essay-status">Coming soon</span>
  </div>

</div>

<p style="text-align: center; padding: 3rem 0 1rem; color: #6b7280; font-size: 0.9rem;">
More projects in progress.
</p>
```
